title: primula × digenea subsp. rothmaleri
intro: Primula × digenea subsp. rothmaleri is a rare and endemic plant species that is a hybrid of Primula digenea and another unknown species. It is also known as a type of primrose, a flowering plant commonly used in herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Primula × digenea subsp. rothmaleri include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and other respiratory problems.
